behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/LLM-DEP: failure is in diarization quality from a real speech model; a stub transcript with perfect labels would hide it. REAL-INFRA also exists (real API), but Agentic is higher because correctness depends on model output content. Adversarial critique: if this only checked field pass-through of already-labeled text, Integration would be enough. Top2 contrast: Agentic beats System because label correctness (not transport) is the risk; this flips to System if behavior is narrowed to API timeout/retry/status handling only.
behaviors.test_description: Run real multi-speaker meeting audio through the production STT provider and assert speaker labels are preserved well enough for attendee attribution (e.g., labeled turns map to known attendees and summary attributions match those turns). Repeat across recordings to catch model variance; do not mock STT output.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is local parsing/structuring logic and can fail inside one function/object boundary. No REAL-INFRA or LLM-DEP required by behavior text. Adversarial critique: if parser correctness depends on tokenizer/schema adapters across modules, Integration could be needed. Top2 contrast: Unit beats Integration because crafted transcript fixtures can directly exercise extraction rules; this flips to Integration if failures only appear when parser output is transformed by a separate mapper before summary input.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixture transcripts containing explicit assignments (including edge punctuation/formatting) and assert exact assignee/task fields in the summary-input object. Keep all dependencies in-process.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: coverage of action items per assignee depends on summarizer model decisions; deterministic stubs would always pass and hide omission failures. Not Workflow by default because behavior can be validated against a provided transcript in one summarization decision. Rejection-gate for Workflow: behavior text does not require validating multiple agentic handoff stages, only final live summary coverage. Adversarial critique: if tested only as a post-processing count check on fixed model output, Integration could suffice. Top2 contrast: Agentic beats Workflow because one model output is the failure boundary; this flips to Workflow if requirement explicitly includes STT->summary chained validation in same test.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript inputs with known per-attendee assignments, call real summarization model, and assert each assigned attendee has at least one action item in live output. Run multiple trials to detect stochastic omission.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text scopes to routing logic after decisions are already identified; this is deterministic mapping and does not need real infra or model output. Adversarial critique: if routing bug only appears when extractor and serializer modules interact, Integration may be minimum. Top2 contrast: Unit beats Integration because direct input of identified-decision objects can verify target schema fields; this flips to Integration if schema enforcement occurs only after module-to-module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Pass pre-identified decision objects into routing function/object and assert they populate `decisions` schema fields and are excluded from narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: deciding what is a key decision and placing it correctly is model-output quality; mocks hide this. Could involve multiple modules, but core failure is model judgment. Rejection-gate for Workflow: behavior references transcript->live summary outcome, not explicitly multi-agentic staged handoff. Adversarial critique: if the model is bypassed and only deterministic formatter placement is tested, Integration/Unit would be enough. Top2 contrast: Agentic beats Workflow because one summarization decision can satisfy behavior; this flips to Workflow if system summarizes per segment with model calls then re-summarizes globally and placement failures arise only across that chain.
behaviors.test_description: Provide real transcripts with explicit decision statements, call real summarization model, and assert those decisions appear in `decisions` section and not only narrative text. Evaluate across multiple runs/samples.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP: this requires chained processing across segments with repeated model calls and final merge coherence; failures occur at handoffs between agentic steps. REAL-INFRA may also matter, but Workflow is higher boundary. Adversarial critique: if only validating deterministic split/merge orchestration with mocked model outputs, Integration/System could be enough. Top2 contrast: Workflow beats Agentic because multiple model decisions are sequenced and merged; this flips to Agentic if architecture changes to one-shot summarization with a single model call.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through full segmented path (segment STT/summarization, sequential processing, merge), then assert all segment-level action items/decisions survive and final summary remains globally coherent (no dropped/contradictory sections).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit end-to-end pipeline behavior with multiple agentic stages (STT then LLM summary) plus real infrastructure. By definition, minimum failure boundary is full workflow. Adversarial critique: if scope were reduced to transport reliability and status transitions only, System could be enough. Top2 contrast: Workflow beats System because success depends on chained model stages completing and producing consumable handoffs; this flips to System if models are replaced with deterministic fixtures and only infra execution is asserted.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like run on a real meeting file: upload, real STT call, real summarization call, structured delivery. Assert completion state, no human retry/manual patching, and valid structured schema at output.
